visa_types.name: Visiting Relatives and Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
Short-term visa (Visiting Relatives & Friends) 
Samples of documents mentioned in the check list “Visiting Relatives and Friends”:
- Information about old passports
- Invitation from Norway - Invitasjon fra referanseperson i Norge
- Guarantee form - Garantiskjema for besøk
- Sponsorship letter
(in case of being sponsored by a person residing in Russia)

To  the attention of applicants submitting documents in Arkhangelsk:

                  Please, click here to view the list of requisite documents. Please, refer to the  section “Documents Required”. Additional information form that needs to be  filled by all applicants except children under 14 can be found in the section  “Download Form”.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Visiting Relatives and Friends]
*Nationals of Moldova and Ukraine holding biometric passports do not need visa for short stay in Schengen zone. 
For more information about fees for residence and work permits, please refer to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/).
Service Fee of Visa Application Centre:
Please Note: There is a service fee of visa application centre levied per application over and above the visa fees: 
- 1837,50 RUB
Exemption from service fee: children under the age of 6 and disabled people. 
Exemption from visa fee: Some categories are exempted from paying the visa fee according to the bilateral Agreement between the Kingdom of Norway and Russian Federation (https://udiregelverk.no/en/documents/bilateral-agreements/2007-06-08e/). Please note that all applicants must pay the visa fee online before submission of documents. Those applicants who are exempt from paying the visa fee will get a refund once the documents have been received by the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Moscow. The amount will be returned to the card from which the fee was withdrawn.
Note: Visa fees are not refundable regardless of the outcome of the visa application.
visa_types.processing_time:
For citizens of the Russian Federation:
Decision is taken within 10 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. Processing time can be extended up to 30 working days, especially if  additional assessment is required.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
For  citizens of other countries:
Decision is taken within 15 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. In certain cases applications are forwarded to the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI). It can take up to 30 days to receive an  answer. In rare cases UDI takes decisions within 60 days.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
Important  note: applications for Schengen visa of citizens of certain countries (https://www.norway.no/ru/russia/norway-russia/news-events2/moscow/news/schengenkonsultasjon/) are  forwarded for agreement to other Member States. It means that other Schengen  countries need to provide their consent before the Norwegian Embassy in Moscow  grants a visa. 
More information on processing times can be found on official website of  the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI): http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/ (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For applicants visiting their family and friends who are legally residing in Norway.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For applicants visiting their family and friends who are legally residing in Norway. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Visiting Relatives and Friends]
*Nationals of Moldova and Ukraine holding biometric passports do not need visa for short stay in Schengen zone. 
For more information about fees for residence and work permits, please refer to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/).
Service Fee of Visa Application Centre:
Please Note: There is a service fee of visa application centre levied per application over and above the visa fees: 
- 1837,50 RUB
Exemption from service fee: children under the age of 6 and disabled people. 
Exemption from visa fee: Some categories are exempted from paying the visa fee according to the bilateral Agreement between the Kingdom of Norway and Russian Federation (https://udiregelverk.no/en/documents/bilateral-agreements/2007-06-08e/). Please note that all applicants must pay the visa fee online before submission of documents. Those applicants who are exempt from paying the visa fee will get a refund once the documents have been received by the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Moscow. The amount will be returned to the card from which the fee was withdrawn.
Note: Visa fees are not refundable regardless of the outcome of the visa application.

## Documents Required

Short-term visa (Visiting Relatives & Friends) 
Samples of documents mentioned in the check list “Visiting Relatives and Friends”:
- Information about old passports
- Invitation from Norway - Invitasjon fra referanseperson i Norge
- Guarantee form - Garantiskjema for besøk
- Sponsorship letter
(in case of being sponsored by a person residing in Russia)

To  the attention of applicants submitting documents in Arkhangelsk:

                  Please, click here to view the list of requisite documents. Please, refer to the  section “Documents Required”. Additional information form that needs to be  filled by all applicants except children under 14 can be found in the section  “Download Form”. 

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the six past months.
- One  photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the Cover  Letter printed out from the Application Portal.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The  head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should  measure about 3 cm
 Photo Appearance 
- The  photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing  the camera directly.
- The  face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The  photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark,  busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In  general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown  from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from  hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses  or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required  for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A  photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does  not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. 

## Processing Time
For citizens of the Russian Federation:
Decision is taken within 10 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. Processing time can be extended up to 30 working days, especially if  additional assessment is required.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
For  citizens of other countries:
Decision is taken within 15 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. In certain cases applications are forwarded to the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI). It can take up to 30 days to receive an  answer. In rare cases UDI takes decisions within 60 days.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
Important  note: applications for Schengen visa of citizens of certain countries (https://www.norway.no/ru/russia/norway-russia/news-events2/moscow/news/schengenkonsultasjon/) are  forwarded for agreement to other Member States. It means that other Schengen  countries need to provide their consent before the Norwegian Embassy in Moscow  grants a visa. 
More information on processing times can be found on official website of  the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI): http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/ (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Form
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/) Please bring the printed receipt with signatures and provide the following details on the receipt:
- Visa Category/Purpose of travel
- Date of travel
- Date of exit
- Number of entries requested.
- Contact number
Guarantee Form for Visits – Must be filled out by the sponsor and stamped by the Police in Norway as an alternative to presenting proof of own financial means. Please click here to download the form .
Invitation from the inviting party in Norway (Invitasjon fra  referanseperson i Norge) – Must be in original and completed and signed by the  sponsor in Norway. Please click here to download the form . 

## To Download a Form:

- Click on the
 
form
 
link
- When the form opens, save the form on your
 
computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select
 
Save Target As
 
from the list
- Save
 
the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Short Term Schengen visa can also be applied for the below purpose of visits:
- Members of official delegations, including holders of Russian service passports,
- Participants in scientific, cultural and artistic activities,
- Participants in international sports events and accompanying persons in their professional capacity,
- Private property owners and their close relatives
- Visitors of military or civil burial grounds
- Other visits.
Any other short stay purpose of visit to Norway that does not qualify in any of the previous categories may be specified in the application form and supported with suitable documents.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Short Term Schengen visa can also be applied for the below purpose of visits:
- Members of official delegations, including holders of Russian service passports,
- Participants in scientific, cultural and artistic activities,
- Participants in international sports events and accompanying persons in their professional capacity,
- Private property owners and their close relatives
- Visitors of military or civil burial grounds
- Other visits.
Any other short stay purpose of visit to Norway that does not qualify in any of the previous categories may be specified in the application form and supported with suitable documents.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Other Visits]
*Nationals of Moldova and Ukraine holding biometric passports do not need visa for short stay in Schengen zone. 
For more information about fees for residence and work permits, please refer to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/).
Service Fee of Visa Application Centre:
Please Note: There is a service fee of visa application centre levied per application over and above the visa fees: 
- 1837,50 RUB
Exemption from service fee: children under the age of 6 and disabled people. 
Exemption from visa fee: Some categories are exempted from paying the visa fee according to the bilateral Agreement between the Kingdom of Norway and Russian Federation (https://udiregelverk.no/en/documents/bilateral-agreements/2007-06-08e/). Please note that all applicants must pay the visa fee online before submission of documents. Those applicants who are exempt from paying the visa fee will get a refund once the documents have been received by the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Moscow. The amount will be returned to the card from which the fee was withdrawn.
Note: Visa fees are not refundable regardless of the outcome of the visa application.

## Documents Required

Culture, Science, International sports events 
Samples of documents mentioned in the check list “Culture, Science, International sports events”:
- Information about old passports
- Sponsorship letter

To  the attention of applicants submitting documents in Arkhangelsk:

                  Please, click to view the list of requisite documents. Please, refer to the  section “Documents Required”. Additional information form that needs to be  filled by all applicants except children under 14 can be found in the section  “Download Form”. 

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the six past months.
- One  photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the Cover  Letter printed out from the Application Portal.

The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:

- The  face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The  photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark,  busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In  general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown  from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from  hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses  or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required  for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A  photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does  not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. 

## Processing Time
For citizens of the Russian Federation:
Decision is taken within 10 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. Processing time can be extended up to 30 working days, especially if  additional assessment is required.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
For  citizens of other countries:
Decision is taken within 15 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. In certain cases applications are forwarded to the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI). It can take up to 30 days to receive an  answer. In rare cases UDI takes decisions within 60 days.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
Important  note: applications for Schengen visa of citizens of certain countries (https://www.norway.no/ru/russia/norway-russia/news-events2/moscow/news/schengenkonsultasjon/) are  forwarded for agreement to other Member States. It means that other Schengen  countries need to provide their consent before the Norwegian Embassy in Moscow  grants a visa. 
More information on processing times can be found on official website of  the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI): http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/ (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa provides for negotiations, talks or other commercial activity or events held in Norway. The visit should not exceed 90 days in any 180 days period.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa provides for negotiations, talks or other commercial activity or events held in Norway. The visit should not exceed 90 days in any 180 days period. 

## Visa Fees

Overview
Tourist visa is given to those visiting Schengen area for tourism/leisure purposes.

Note: Visa fees are not refundable regardless of the outcome of the visa application.

## Documents Required

Short-term visa (Tourism and other private visits)
 
Samples of documents mentioned in the check list “Tourism and other private visits”:
- Information about old passports
- Itinerary plan
- Sponsorship letter

To  the attention of applicants submitting documents in Arkhangelsk:

                  Please, click here to view the list of requisite documents. Please, refer to the  section “Documents Required”. Additional information form that needs to be  filled by all applicants except children under 14 can be found in the section  “Download Form”. 

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the six past months.
- One  photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the Cover  Letter printed out from the Application Portal.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The  head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should  measure about 3 cm
 Photo Appearance 
- The  photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing  the camera directly.
- The  face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The  photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark,  busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In  general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown  from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from  hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses  or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required  for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A  photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does  not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. 

## Processing Time
For citizens of the Russian Federation:
Decision is taken within 10 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. Processing time can be extended up to 30 working days, especially if  additional assessment is required.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
For  citizens of other countries:
Decision is taken within 15 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. In certain cases applications are forwarded to the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI). It can take up to 30 days to receive an  answer. In rare cases UDI takes decisions within 60 days.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
Important  note: applications for Schengen visa of citizens of certain countries (https://www.norway.no/ru/russia/norway-russia/news-events2/moscow/news/schengenkonsultasjon/) are  forwarded for agreement to other Member States. It means that other Schengen  countries need to provide their consent before the Norwegian Embassy in Moscow  grants a visa. 
More information on processing times can be found on official website of  the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI): http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/ (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Form
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/) Please bring the printed receipt with signatures and provide the following details on the receipt:
- Visa Category/Purpose of travel
- Date of travel
- Date of exit
- Number of entries requested.
- Contact number
Guarantee Form for Visits – Must be filled out by the sponsor and stamped by the Police in Norway as an alternative to presenting proof of own financial means. Please click here to download the form.
Invitation from the inviting party in Norway (Invitasjon fra  referanseperson i Norge) – Must be in original and completed and signed by the  sponsor in Norway. Please click here to download the form . 

## To Download a Form:

- Click on the
 
form
 
link
- When the form opens, save the form on your
 
computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select
 
Save Target As
 
from the list
- Save
 
the form on your computer.

visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Study/Exchange programs/Summer schools]
*Nationals of Moldova and Ukraine holding biometric passports do not need visa for short stay in Schengen zone. 
For more information about fees for residence and work permits, please refer to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/).
Service Fee of Visa Application Centre:
Please Note: There is a service fee of visa application centre levied per application over and above the visa fees: 
- 1837,50 RUB
Exemption from service fee: children under the age of 6 and disabled people. 
Exemption from visa fee: Some categories are exempted from paying the visa fee according to the bilateral Agreement between the Kingdom of Norway and Russian Federation (https://udiregelverk.no/en/documents/bilateral-agreements/2007-06-08e/). Please note that all applicants must pay the visa fee online before submission of documents. Those applicants who are exempt from paying the visa fee will get a refund once the documents have been received by the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Moscow. The amount will be returned to the card from which the fee was withdrawn.
Note: Visa fees are not refundable regardless of the outcome of the visa application.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This type of visa provides for short-term studies, courses, exchange programs between educational establishments in Russia and Norway, training programs and summer schools activities for up to 90 days. If the program of course is more than 90 days, you need to apply for a study permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This type of visa provides for short-term studies, courses, exchange programs between educational establishments in Russia and Norway, training programs and summer schools activities for up to 90 days. If the program of course is more than 90 days, you need to apply for a study permit. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Study/Exchange programs/Summer schools]
*Nationals of Moldova and Ukraine holding biometric passports do not need visa for short stay in Schengen zone. 
For more information about fees for residence and work permits, please refer to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/).
Service Fee of Visa Application Centre:
Please Note: There is a service fee of visa application centre levied per application over and above the visa fees: 
- 1837,50 RUB
Exemption from service fee: children under the age of 6 and disabled people. 
Exemption from visa fee: Some categories are exempted from paying the visa fee according to the bilateral Agreement between the Kingdom of Norway and Russian Federation (https://udiregelverk.no/en/documents/bilateral-agreements/2007-06-08e/). Please note that all applicants must pay the visa fee online before submission of documents. Those applicants who are exempt from paying the visa fee will get a refund once the documents have been received by the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Moscow. The amount will be returned to the card from which the fee was withdrawn.
Note: Visa fees are not refundable regardless of the outcome of the visa application.

## Documents Required

Short-term visa (Study/Exchange programs/Summer schools) 
Samples of documents mentioned in the check list “Study/Exchange programs/Summer schools”:
- Information about old passports
- Sponsorship letter

To  the attention of applicants submitting documents in Arkhangelsk:

                  Please, click here to view the list of requisite documents. Please, refer to the  section “Documents Required”. Additional information form that needs to be  filled by all applicants except children under 14 can be found in the section  “Download Form”. 

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the six past months.

- One  photograph must be submitted. The photograph should be glued to the Cover  Letter printed out from the Application Portal.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The  head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should  measure about 3 cm
 Photo Appearance 
- The  photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing  the camera directly.
- The  face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The  photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark,  busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In  general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown  from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from  hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses  or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required  for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A  photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does  not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. 

## Processing Time
For citizens of the Russian Federation:
Decision is taken within 10 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. Processing time can be extended up to 30 working days, especially if  additional assessment is required.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
For  citizens of other countries:
Decision is taken within 15 calendar days* from the moment of receipt of the application by the Norwegian Embassy in  Moscow. In certain cases applications are forwarded to the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI). It can take up to 30 days to receive an  answer. In rare cases UDI takes decisions within 60 days.
*Transit time to/from Moscow not included.
Important  note: applications for Schengen visa of citizens of certain countries (https://www.norway.no/ru/russia/norway-russia/news-events2/moscow/news/schengenkonsultasjon/) are  forwarded for agreement to other Member States. It means that other Schengen  countries need to provide their consent before the Norwegian Embassy in Moscow  grants a visa. 
More information on processing times can be found on official website of  the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI): http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/ (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Form
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/) Please bring the printed receipt with signatures and provide the following details on the receipt:
- Visa Category/Purpose of travel
- Date of travel
- Date of exit
- Number of entries requested.
- Contact number
